body_txt: Pandas know kung fu . Pandas are strongly associated with their native China , and since All Chinese People Know Kung-Fu and All Asians Know Martial Arts , pandas will invariably be skilled in the martial arts. Despite their big size , they will be portrayed as forces to be reckoned with. This trope exists despite, or because of, real-life giant pandas usually being stereotyped as lazy lumps of bamboo-eating cuteness. Sometimes, the Fighting Panda is instead a red panda, a superficially similar but much smaller bamboo-eating carnivoran note A carnivoran in the musteloid family along with weasels, raccoons and their close relatives, rather than the ursid (bear) family. "Panda" referred to them before it referred to the black and white bear. also native to China. A specific type of Bears Are Bad News . Subverts the usual stereotype of pandas as lazy or slow and not nimble animals, but as pandas are typically large anyway, will often overlap with Kevlard and/or Stout Strength . Also a subtrope of Animal Occupation Stereotypes .
